komentar:
Si jendral dipasang pasak berupa pedang pada masa sekigahara (1600an) oleh si rubah. Kemudian dilakukan ritual jadi bala tentara iblis si rubah tapi lokasi masih di Jepang belum dikubur di Korea.
Nah setelah Jepang menjajah Korea, si rubah baru pindah ke Korea membawa si jendral. Dan kemudian di kubur di sana di tahun 1900an.
Harus diketahui, bahwa Jepang pernah menyerang Korea tahun 1600an pada masa Toyotomi Hideyoshi, namun gagal. Ini terjadi sebelum perang Sekigahara. Nah si jendral ini anggota western army yang ikut menyerang Korea. Tapi balik ke Jepang setelah gagal. Dia baru kalah dan mati di perang Sekigahara setelah itu.

si setan samurai ini kan aslinya gabungan antara pedang terkutuk sama manusia

jadi pas tahun 1600 atau lebih lama lagi, dia wujudnya masih berupa pedang dan setelah masa perang antar clan berakhir dia disimpen di kuil (Namsan shrine, Daikotu)

sampe akhirnya diakhir periode perang Jepang-Korea, pedang itu diambil sama dukun Jepang (Fox Shaman Murayama Junji) buat ritual bangkitin si setan samurai ini, sebagai upaya terakhir mereka bikin tiang yg gak bisa dicabut.

jadi orang yg dipenggal pas ritual itu bukan badan aslinya.

komentar: Film exhuma berbeda dgn film horror pada umunya yg mengutaman adegan jumpscare, exhuma mengangkat misteri dari sisi lain, seperti keterkaitan Sejarah antara Korea Selatan dengan Jepang, Sejarah mengenai pasak besi yang dipercaya Sebagian Masyarakat korea Selatan. Film exhuma membahas hal2 yang dekat dengan Masyarakat korea sendiri, seperti : Fengshui, perdukunan, kepercayaan hujan yg dapat membuat roh lama pergi ke akhirat, melibatkan kepercayaan dari berbagai agama, hwa rim, bong gil dan biksu mewakili budha, Young Geun ( Si Penggali kubur) mewakili Kristen, adanya komunitas pasak besi yang mencari pasak besi secara nyata, sedangkan di dalam film pasak besinya di masukkan kedalam tubuh sang jendral, jadi sangat menarik, mereka mencari kemana2 juga tdk ketemu karena pasak besinya ada didalam tubuh jendral jepang, kan jadi masuk akal dikaitkan dengan kehidupan nyata. Di tambah acting pemain yang sangat bagus, wah pokonya ini film sangat layak dan pantas mendapat apresiasi dari Masyarakat.

komentar:
saya lengkapi lagi yah,,
Jadi Awal kisah itu adalah perang Imjin abad 15 antara Keshogunan Jepang,Dinasti joeseon(Korea) dan Dinasti Ming(China).Kekalahan jepang di semenangjung korea membuat marah Shogun dan menyuruh Biksu Gisune mengutuk tanah Korea agar terus terjadi huru hara dan kekacauan.dukun gisune menggunakan jasad Panglima Jepang yang tewas untuk menanam pasak besi ,dari sinilah muncul istilah rubah menikam punggum harimau.Siluman rubah (Gisune) menanam pasang bumi di semenanjung korea yang secara fenshui adalah harimau.
Nah..400 Tahun Kemudian Di abab 19 era shogun di jepang sudah berakhir di ganti era Kaisar Meiji.nah di tahun 1900 awal jepang dgn tentara modern nya kembali menginvasi korea dan memanfaatkan raja2 boneka korea termasuk leluhurnya keluarga kaya raya ini.
di masa ini Gisune (Siluman rubah) kembali muncul setelah 400 tahun dan menyamar menjadi seorang biksu kepercayaan kaisar jepang.
kemudian memanipulasi agar raja boneka ini dikubur di tanah pasak bumi 400 tahun lalu untuk menyamarkan keberadaan pasak bumi yang selama ini membuat korea kacau balau.
nah..kakek keluarga ini roh nya tersiksa di makamkan di tanah pasak bumi ini,sehingga menghantui keturunannya,tp krn100 tahun tidak di gubris yang tersisa dari roh kakek ini hanya kebencian dan hawa negatif.
jadi hantu samurai itu adalah perwujudan perang imjin makanya hantu nya banyak membahasa kisah 500 tahun lalu,
sementara hantu kakek adalah gambaran pendudukan jepang di korea abad 19,makanya pas kepala keluarga kesurupan di dekat jendela ucapannya sepertyi penghormatan kepada kaisar jepang abad 19 bukan lagi bicara shogun di masa lampau.
dari seragam nya saja sudah beda antara hantu samurai dengan dengan pakaian militer jepang di foto kakek buyut keluarga itu krn beda masa.
Jadi Gisune ini adalah siluman yang ada di abad 16 dan 19 yang membenci dan mengutuk korea dengan istilah rubah melukai punngung harimau..

komentar:
Baru sempet nonton kemaren, setelah dicerna dan dipikir2in, tema film ini yang paling kental terasa adalah "Yin dan Yang". 
Seperti halnya FengShui yang jadi elemen utama film ini, keseimbangan dan harmoni dari hal yang kontradiktif itu merupakan kunci segalanya: 
First part berasa Japanese onryou movie dengan tema Korea, second part berasa Korean creature feature dengan tema Jepang
Hubungan antara Korea & Jepang - yang dijajah dan penjajah. 
Hwa-Rim orang Korea yang mengerti & belajar shamanisme Jepang
Bong Gil yang menato sutra Buddhist di badan, tp bergaya modern 
Mr Kim yang jago FengShui. Old school, idealis, tapi open minded dan kreatif
Yeong-Geun si undertaker lihai, demen duit tapi sering mengutip ayat Alkitab
Si Kakek yang menjual negaranya demi keuntungan dan menerima hukuman 
Si Shogun yang rela dijadiin tumbal projek hitobashira demi melayani negaranya

@deocaesario3315  hito itu orang, bashira itu hashira (pillar seperti di Demon's Slayer). Dengan kata lain "hitobashira" itu seperti di Indonesia yg dibilang tumbal projek, anak2 atau orang dewasa yang disemen hidup2 jadi pilar biar gedung atau jembatannya kuat. 

Kalau menurut saya jendral itu kebalikannya, bukannya jadi kuat malah jadi melemahkan dan bikin kacau tempat  dia jadi pilar nya itu. Jendral itu dimasukkan katana yang mewakilkan elemen besi ke tubuhnya, lalu dia dikubur vertikal di titik "punggung harimau" itu.

Sementara abad 19 jepang kembali menginvasi korea dgn tentara modern disitu ada raja boneka jepanh ,,gisune ini siluman yg ada di abad 16 dan 19..gisune memanipulasi agar raja itu di kubur di atas pasak abad 16 untuk menyamarkan..makanya roh kakek yg di kubur 100 tahun berontak menghantui keturunannya agar dipindahkan tp krn sudah 100 tahun lamanya yg tersisa hanya kebencian dan hawa negatif..
Itulah istilaj rubah melukai pinggang harimau artinya siluman rubah (gisune) menusuk semenanjung korea agar terkutuk penuh huru hara termasuk pecahnya korut dan korsel..

komentar:
Punten Bang, mau nambahin sedikit soal Nure-Onna + teoriku dikit ya. Jadi Nure-Onna menurut folklore dr daerah Iwami, adalah yokai (semacem siluman) yg berkaitan dengan Ushi-Oni untuk menjebak manusia. Cara menjebaknya adalah dengan ngasih bayi yang akan berubah menjadi batu, menyatu dengan tangan manusia yg megang bayi itu. Nah di saat itu, Ushi-oni akan muncul untuk menyerang manusia yg megang batu bayi itu. 

Kalo menurut saya, keberadaan Nure-Onna di sana bukan hanya sbg kutukan, tapi sebagai penanda bagi si oni untuk bangun, krn ada yg mengusik makam itu. Baru kepikiran karena  penggali yg diserang cuma org yg menggal kepala Nure-Onna itu (intinya org itu udah di tandain sbg penerima malapetaka, sekaligus peringatan buat yg lain untuk ga macem2 di burial ground itu). Oni jg dipercaya sbg pembawa malapetaka. 


Soal api banaspati itu sih, saya cuma kepikiran kalo will-o-wisp itu dalam bahasa Jepang disebut sebagai Onibi (demon fire), pake kanji Oni dan api. Soalnya Oni itu lebih idientik dengan petir dan halilintar. 


Mengenai Gisune, menurut saya dia dibuat berdasarkan sosok Abe no Seimei, sosok onmyouji (ahli fengsui dari Jepang) yg sangat ternama di Jepang. Latar belakangnya sama persis sama dgn legenda Seimei yg lahir dari perkawinan manusia dan kitsune. Seimei sendiri terlahir jenius, dr umur 5 taun udah bisa bikin oni jinak. 

Kalo ada salah, mohon dimaafkan 🙏

komentar:
Saya setuju sih dengan yang sebagian besar disampaikan oleh Mas Rasyid. Cuman mau nambahin aja sedikit. Menurut saya pribadi, film ini tidak hanya membahas soal kolonialisme tapi juga kapitalisme yang masuk ke Korea karena kolonialisasi oleh Jepang tadi. Di bagian awal, keempat karakter kita sibuk berbicara soal uang besar yang menjadi upah bagi mereka nantinya jika berhasil. Mereka berbicara untung dan rugi. Dan ketika di babak ketiga ketika mereka bertiga bertengkar di rumah sakit (Karena yang temennya hwarim kan lagi di ranjang rumah sakit), mereka akhirnya sepakat untuk bekerja sama tanpa uang. ada dialog di antara mereka yang mengesampingkan soal uang dulu. Dan di poin inilah mereka sadar bahwa ada hal yang lebih penting daripada uang, yaitu keluarga (persahabatan). Oleh karena itu tidak heran di ending, karakter yang diperankan oleh Choi Min Sik mengajak mereka berfoto bersama di pernikahan anaknya sebagai keluarga. 

rubah-rubah yang ada di pemakaman itu sebenarnya lebih menyimbolkan keempat karakter utama ini sih. Bagaimana mereka memanfaatkan uang orang-orang kaya untuk kepentingan mereka sendiri. Mewakili mereka yang juga licik. Inilah mengapa, menurut saya, film ini bagus karena bisa secara subtle merajut isu ini di dalam cerita tanpa sedikitpun terasa kalau si filmmakernya lagi bahas tentang ini.
clean: setuju sih dentidakn mas rasyid cuman nambahin aja pribadi film tidak membahas kolonialisme jutidak kapitalisme masuk korea kolonialisasi jepang keempat karakter sibuk berbicara uang upah berhasil berbicara untung rugi babak ketitidak bertitidak bertengkar rumah sakit temennya hwarim ranjang rumah sakit sepakat uang dialog mengesampingkan uang poin sadar uang keluartidak persahabatan tidak heran ending karakter diperankan choi min sik mentidakjak berfoto pernikahan anaknya sebatidaki keluartidak rubahrubah pemakaman menyimbolkan keempat karakter utama sih batidakimana memanfaatkan uang orangorang kaya kepentintidakn mewakili jutidak licik mentidakpa film bagus subtle merajut isu cerita sedikitpun si filmmakernya bahas

Buat yg bingung ttg Shogun di jepang pada masa kekaisaran itu ada 4 posisi/kasta yaitu:
-Emperor alias kaisar
-Shogun
-Daimyo/samurai
-peasants

komentar: Saya malah pikir gisune itu kitsune (siluman rubah ekor sembilan) dalam mitologi Jepang. Jumlah rubah yang jaga di makam juga ada sembilan. Makanya kata-katanya cakar rubah melukai pinggang harimau (cmiiw harimau gunung baekdu itu salah satu representasi KorSel). Saya lihatnya KorSel udah mulai berani bahas keburukan Jepang di beberapa film-nya karena pernah dijajah. Bahkan ada kalimat "Setan Jepang akan membunuh siapapun bahkan yang tidak bersalah." Di Conjuring 2 antagonis sekundernya si hantu kakek (lupa namanya) dan antagonis primernya justru si Valak. Di Exhuma pun sama. Peti mati pertama yang di ekshumasi milik leluhur keluarga Park itu bukan musuh terberat para protagonisnya. Justru peti yang dikubur dibawahnya lagi yang posisinya aneh (vertikal) berisi jasad iblis samurai raksasa yang jadi antagonis utamanya.

Asli ini film seram bagi saya padahal saya hobi nonton film horror setiap hari sepulang kerja. Rating 8/10 lah.

Not that I'm trying to defend Japanese for what they have done in the past, but growing up watching Chinese dramas full of that hate (my country entertainment sucks back then), along with hundreds verions of Journey to the West, and Legend of Condor Heroes, got me so sick of these.

komentar:
The Grandmother's exchange with the samurai has cultural significance. 
The “samurai” or Japanese spirit (or demon/ghost general) is bound by spiritual rules and affected by the traditional Korean shamanistic beliefs, rituals, and power structures. In many traditions, especially Shamanic and folk religions in Korea (and neighboring cultures), there is a belief that ancestral spirits or local deities must be respected, and invading or violent spirits (especially “foreign” ones) must avoid direct confrontation with those guardians. 

The grandmother figure serves as a manifestation of that local sacred order. When she appears, it signals that this sacred order is at play. she is a respected shamanic figure whose spirit carries authority and spiritual power. Ghosts or spirits like the samurai / Anima are bound by spiritual hierarchy, especially in this film’s universe. The character of the grandmother invokes protection and spiritual displacement, a power strong enough to repel or disrupt the samurai’s attacks.

komentar:
So some folklore stuff about Professor Kim's comment on Foxes and Graves. There's likely there's a mix of Japanese and Korean in the movie, I'm not versed enough to pinpoint what is what but I'll leave something interesting.

So Foxes in particular range from divine messengers of the Gods to bloodthirsty succubi preying on souls in Japanese and Korean beliefs. Now there could be two reasons why foxes and graves are a bad thing. Reason 1 is the gumiho, a Korean fox spirit which is said to wander grave sites to dig up corpses to eat their heart or liver(depends on the story) in order to become human or become more powerful. Another reason and maybe this is why the Professor Kim is confused, might be leaning into Buddhist beliefs(Wu Xing is a Taoist belief mostly) of the goddess Dakini, who in Japanese beliefs would wound up being associated with Inari called Dakiniten. Dakiniten is a corpse eater who worked for Enma's court(the 5th Judge of Naraka), and her foxes are her eyes and ears to find the dead who are still in the mortal realms. It could be that the foxes sensed the spirit of the samurai, intending to retrieve it but likely Gisune's ritual prevents them from bringing him back to Japan.

All in all, if you kinda ignore Exhuma for its horror factor, it kinda makes a really cool supernatural thriller like Ghostbusters or Trese.

komentar:
It’s somewhat very relatable, even in Indonesia, we believe that during the Japanese occupation, they put all these iron stakes all around the country to create spritual unrest, disturbing fengshui and create division/distrust among people (for example the border between South and North Korea, so they will forever be enemies).

After Exhuma was released, I started hearing some story in Indonesia too, in the Borneo Island, near the border between 3 countries; Malaysia, Indonesia and Brunei. It was told that some developer company wanted to create a resort near the area, but was kept being ‘disturbed’. There was an old, ancient tree located at the center of the area. Locals had already told them not to linger around the tree area, let alone cut the tree, because it was haunted by a malevolent Japanese geisha spirit.

They tried multiple offerings, as far as doing human sacrifices to the tree, yet nothing seemed to worked. One of the shaman they hired told them the tree needed to be cut, if they want to proceed. Strangely enough, the tree was immune to everything (axe, fire, chemicals, etc), either tools were broken or the worker got possessed and died. So they tried the other way; to dig up the tree. Turns out, there was a vertical coffin underneath, chained with iron barbs and difficult to open.

It was a bloody story, as a lot of people died, possessed, even the shamans they tried to hire. Long story short, when the coffin was finally opened, inside was a skeleton with iron stakes, wearing a kimono. Eventually, they managed to poison the tree which made the soil surrounding the area infertile, and also causing the resort project being abandoned. Some speculate maybe due to lack of funds after so many failures, some also said that because the area was practically ‘dead’ and ominous, it wasn’t a good place to be developed anymore.

The rumors told that the geisha was meant to be an entertainment for the Japanese soldiers dispatched there, but somehow tragically killed/being abused to death, so they used her body as a stake. She build resentment over this, making the area unhabitable and haunted. Creepy story.

komentar:
Certainly! Nure-onna is a fascinating figure in Japanese folklore, known for her distinctive and eerie appearance, as well as her varied stories and roles in myth.

### Appearance
Nure-onna is typically depicted with the head of a woman and the body of a large snake. Her hair is often described as long and black, and she is sometimes seen holding a bundle that resembles a swaddled baby. The juxtaposition of her human head and serpentine body creates a striking and unsettling image.

### Name and Characteristics
The name "Nure-onna" translates to "wet woman," referencing her association with water and her perpetually wet appearance. Her hair and body are always damp, adding to her eerie, otherworldly presence.

### Legends and Behavior
Nure-onna is commonly found near rivers, lakes, and seashores. She has various behaviors and intentions in different legends:

1. **Luring Victims**: In some stories, Nure-onna uses her bundle, which appears to be a baby, to attract unsuspecting travelers. When someone offers to help her or take the bundle, it becomes incredibly heavy, preventing the victim from escaping. Nure-onna then attacks, sometimes draining the victim's blood or simply drowning them.

2. **Washing Hair**: In other tales, Nure-onna is seen washing her hair by the water. Those who interrupt her or come too close might face her wrath. This motif is common in yokai lore, where disturbing a supernatural being often leads to dire consequences.

3. **Guardian Role**: Not all stories portray Nure-onna as malevolent. In some versions, she is a guardian of sacred sites or treasures. People who approach her with respect and offer her assistance might be spared or even rewarded.

### Cultural Impact
Nure-onna, like many yokai, embodies the fear and mystery of the natural world, particularly the dangers associated with water. Her stories serve as cautionary tales, warning people to be wary of their surroundings and to treat the unknown with respect.

### Related Yokai
Nure-onna is part of a larger category of snake-woman yokai in Japanese folklore. Another well-known example is **Yamata-no-Orochi**, a giant serpent with multiple heads. While Orochi is more monstrous and less human, both figures illustrate the blending of human and serpentine characteristics in Japanese myth.

### Modern Interpretations
Nure-onna continues to appear in various forms of media, including literature, manga, anime, and video games. She represents the enduring fascination with yokai in Japanese culture and their ability to adapt to contemporary storytelling.

In summary, Nure-onna is a complex and multifaceted yokai whose stories reflect themes of danger, mystery, and the supernatural. Her presence in Japanese folklore underscores the cultural significance of yokai and their role in conveying moral and cautionary tales.

As far as I know it, this movie is mesmerizing. It's not necessarily scary— at least for me, and to be honest the first time I watched it I don't really like the first act, it's not bad at any mean it's good but The sinister titled chapter of "The Unnamed Grave" met with an ordinary ghost of an old Man was . . . Kinda a let down.

But then suddenly that plot just dropped at the start of the second act, caught me off guard. And when they pulled that inhumanly tall coffin, my face just lift up from my palm. And by that point the movie became threatening, and boy does it really threatened you. Suddenly there's demon, and one of the character that I feel had the strongest presence suddenly get badly injured and literally strip away out of his own control. It really pays off the flaw of first act, and that's just a really good writing. 

It really emphasize the play out of the story, it manage to explain itself on why the story need to be that way on the first act. The character who used to not have to ponder and thinking too deep, even still act stiff and hard shelled minutes before the demon showed up, suddenly their confidence on the first act just got strip down within minutes, forced to work with whatever they have, improvising, while searching for solutions, and the worst thing is . . . Things get more complicated every seconds in. It gives you the impact, and the impact was as strong as any cargo truck in any anime's cliché, and it really rewarding me for a second watch. 

Not a scary movie by any means necessary, but it's mesmerizingly threatening. A really solid watch.

komentar: When I watched this and I saw how they're so many religious themes (e.g. Christianity, Shamanism, Buddhism) mixed together, I immediately knew it'd be the same director as the director of Svaha the Sixth Finger (Jang Jae Hyun) since he really likes to mix in the topic of faith. It's so fun and interesting to watch! It has pretty similar characteristics to the wailing as well (again, mix of religions and beliefs... and for some reason Japanese demons, kind of, haha). The cinematography of these movies were incredible and it seems to me that Korean horror movies often have a great message rather than being just pure scare, and are usually very plot/drama focused which still goes beautifully with "scary scenes". I'd recommend it, I didn't think it was too scary but more of a beautiful story than anything. I would recommend Svaha the Sixth Finger too by the same producer if you're interested, but that wasn't really scary (more like it gives a sensation of doom) as far as I remember. There weren't any "ghosts" or "demons" as you'd expect.

hokigami: Appearance: A hahakigami is a tsukumogami which takes up residence in a broom. They can sometimes be seen on cold, windy late autumn mornings, sweeping wildly at the blowing leaves.

Origin: Long ago, brooms were not household cleaning tools, but actually holy instruments used in ritual purification ceremonies. They were used to on the air in a room or area in order to purify it and sweep out any evil spirits and negative energy that might be lingering there. Like any tool used for many years, a broom which reaches a very old age becomes a perfect home for a spirit — perhaps even more so in the case of a hahakigami because of the ritual nature of its origin.

Hahakigami are used also as magical charms for safe and quick childbirth. Because brooms are used to “sweep out” evil energy, a hahakigami acts as a sort of totem to “sweep out” the baby from its mother safely. They are also used as charms to keep guests from overstaying their visit. Anyone who has stayed beyond their welcome might also be “swept out” by the power of the hahakigami. komentar:
Jadi intinya dulu itu ada pejuang jepang yg tewas disana, nah beberapa orang memakamkannya dengan pasak besi(katana) ditubuhnya dan dimakamkan dengan ritual khusus biar rohnya gak keluar balas dendam. 
Nah singkat cerita kakek buyutnya punya temen biksu jepang, lalu si kakek meninggal sementara si Biksu menyarankan dimakamkan disana dengan dalih agar terhindar dari pencurian makam.. 
Kenapa biksunya menyarankan disana, mungkin si biksu tahu bahwa disana ada makam si samurai, dan dia berfikir jasad kakeknya ini bisa jadi wadah untuk roh si samurai. 
kenapa dia bilang ada pencurian makam, karena pasak besi(katana) si samurai ini gaada, jd mungkin aja dia berfikir demikian.
nah singkat cerita pas makam kakek buyutnya dikeluarkan, ada pelanggaran yg dilakukan pak kim dan tim, dimana peti nya dibuka, yg membuat roh samurai langsung menyerang dalam wujud kakek buyut. 
Disana dia juga sempat merasuki si bapak, dan membuat gerakan dan kata kata aneh "rubah melukai punggung harimau" yg merujuk ke jepang yg berhasil menjajah korea, sebelum kepalanya si bapak diputer..
Pas peti di kremasi roh samurai tsb hilang, dia tertarik mundur lagi dan kembali ke peti nya, tapi yg tanpa disadari, pak kim justru membuat kesalahan fatal dengan menemukan dan menggali peti si samurai tsb malah dikeluarkan dibawa ke rumah pendeta.. 
Alhasil roh samurai itu bisa terlepas untuk kedua kalinya, namun kali ini dia dalam wujud aslinya yg lebih kuat. 
ya akhirnya dicari lah pasak besi(katana) yg mempunyai energi magis atau sumber kekuatan si samurai. 
setelah melihat flashback bahwa ternyata pasak besi(katana) ada di tubuh si Samurai, pak kim langsung menyerangnya, tanpa pikir panjang dan membelahnya.. 
barulah disana mereka bisa terbebas dari kutukan Iblis Samurai itu. 

Sekedar info :  Pasak Besi ini merupakan media atau alat magis yg digunakan tentara jepang dulu, jadi dulu banyak tentara jepang yg menanamkan pasak magis ini di negara" yang dijajahnya, dengan maksud. bahwa mereka berhasil menjajah negeri tsb. 
Nah berhubung di Film ini Pasak besi nya malah dimasukan ke tubuh si Samurai, alhasil rohnya dia berubah menjadi Iblis karena kekuatan magis dari pasak besinya

Makanya pas kuburannya digali malah jadi malapetaka
- Terus untuk pasak besinya sendiri ternyata ditanamkan didalam tubuhnya si jenderal siluman itu. Makanya jenderal siluman itu dikubur secara vertikal (seperti pasak pada umumnya).
- Dan silumannya udh gak bisa balik ya, karena udh binasa. Akibat di pukul pake kayu yg dikasih darahnya si ahli fengshui itu. Karena siluman ini tuh kan elemennya besi dan api, lawannya besi itu kayu dan lawannya api itu air. Makanya di lawan pake kayu sama darah (menggantikan air) dia bisa binasa. Begitu
